# Scan GitHub for secrets

Connect GitHub as a data source to HCP Vault Radar to scan your GitHub repositories
for sensitive data and secrets. Vault Radar supports scanning GitHub
Cloud and GitHub Enterprise Server. You can connect to GitHub either as a data source host 
to auto discover GitHub organizations or as a data source tied to a single GitHub organization.

@include 'vault-radar/radar-quickstart-tutorials.mdx'

<Note>

Vault Radar has a limit of 5000 repositories per connection. If you have more
than 5000 repositories, Vault Radar will select the 5000 repositories with the
most recent activity.

</Note>

## Prerequisites

- HCP IAM user with the HCP owner, or admin role
- [Vault Radar agent](/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/deploy) deployed and running (agent scan only)
- For GitHub App authentication with GitHub Enterprise Server or **HCP Vault Radar agent scan**, follow [these instructions](https://github.com/hashicorp-guides/vault-radar-demo/tree/main/github-app) to create a GitHub App, then register it in Vault Radar under **Settings** > **GitHub apps** before adding a data source.
- For authentication with GitHub [personal access token (PAT)](https://github.com/settings/tokens/new?scopes=repo,read:org,admin:repo_hook,admin:org_hook,read:user), create a PAT with the following permission scopes:

  <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

  ```plaintext
  repo > all
  admin:org > read:org
  admin:repo_hook > all
  admin:org_hook > all
  user > read:user
  ```

  </CodeBlockConfig>

## Add a GitHub data source host

You can add and scan GitHub Cloud or GitHub Enterprise Server using the **HCP
Vault Radar cloud scan** or **HCP Vault Radar agent scan**.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="GitHub Cloud">

Add GitHub Cloud as a data source to scan your GitHub organization's repositories for secrets.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar cloud scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**.

1. Select **GitHub Cloud**.

1. Enter the personal access token in the **Enter your GitHub personal
   access token** field.

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar agent scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

1. Select **GitHub Cloud**.

1. Enter the path to the environment variable with the personal access token in
   the **Enter environment path to your GitHub personal access token**
   field.
   
   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```plaintext
   env://GITHUB_TOKEN
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>
<Tab heading="GitHub Enterprise Server">

Add GitHub Enterprise Server as a data source to scan your GitHub organization's repositories for secrets.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar cloud scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**.

1. Select **GitHub Enterprise Server**.

1. Enter the following information:
   
   - **Enter your GitHub domain name:** Fully qualified domain name of
     your GitHub Enterprise Server.
   - **Enter your GitHub personal access token:** Access token from your GitHub Enterprise
     Server.

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar agent scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

1. Select **GitHub Enterprise Server**.

1. Enter the following information:
   
   - **Enter your GitHub domain name:** Fully qualified domain name of
     your GitHub Enterprise Server.
   - **Enter environment path to your GitHub personal access token:** Access token from your GitHub Enterprise
     Server.
   
   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```plaintext
   env://GITHUB_TOKEN
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Add a GitHub data source

You can add and scan GitHub Cloud or GitHub Enterprise Server using the **HCP
Vault Radar cloud scan** or **HCP Vault Radar agent scan**.

### GitHub App Authentication

GitHub app authentication uses GitHub's short-lived installation access tokens
with scoped permissions, providing more granular access control. Installation
access tokens are well-suited for use cases where long-lived user credentials
are undesirable.

<Note title="GitHub app authentication prerequisite">

You must create and register a GitHub App before completing the steps below.
Follow the [GitHub App setup
instructions](https://github.com/hashicorp-guides/vault-radar-demo/tree/main/github-app)
to create the app, then register it in Vault Radar under **Settings** > **GitHub
apps** before returning here.

</Note>

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data sources**.

1. Click **Add data source**.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan** OR select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

1. Select **GitHub Cloud** OR select **GitHub Enterprise Server**.

1. Under **Select authentication method**, click the dropdown and select **GitHub OAuth** to authenticate with the GitHub App.

1. If using **GitHub Enterprise Server**, select which GitHub App to authenticate with from the **GitHub app** dropdown.

1. Click **Connect to GitHub**.

1. On redirect to GitHub, select which GitHub Org to Onboard and the repositories to install on.

1. On successful redirect to **Vault Radar**, click **Next**.

1. Select either **All active repositories** or **Select repositories to monitor**.

1. Click **Finish**.

### PAT Authentication

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="GitHub Cloud">

Add GitHub Cloud as a data source to scan your GitHub organization's repositories for secrets.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar cloud scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data sources**.

1. Click **Add data source**.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**.

1. Select **GitHub Cloud**.

1. Enter the personal access token in the **Enter your GitHub personal
   access token** field.

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All active repositories** or **Select repositories to monitor**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar agent scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data sources**.

1. Click **Add data source**.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

1. Select **GitHub Cloud**.

1. Enter the path to the environment variable with the personal access token in
   the **Enter environment path to your GitHub personal access token**
   field.
   
   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```plaintext
   env://GITHUB_TOKEN
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>
<Tab heading="GitHub Enterprise Server">

Add GitHub Enterprise Server as a data source to scan your GitHub organization's repositories for secrets.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar cloud scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data sources**.

1. Click **Add data source**.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**.

1. Select **GitHub Enterprise Server**.

1. Enter the following information:
   
   - **Enter your GitHub domain name:** Fully qualified domain name of
     your GitHub Enterprise Server.
   - **Enter your GitHub personal access token:** Access token from your GitHub Enterprise
     Server.

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All active repositories** or **Select repositories to monitor**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar agent scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data sources**.

1. Click **Add data source**.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

1. Select **GitHub Enterprise Server**.

1. Enter the following information:
   
   - **Enter your GitHub domain name:** Fully qualified domain name of
     your GitHub Enterprise Server.
   - **Enter environment path to your GitHub personal access token:** Access token from your GitHub Enterprise
     Server.
   
   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```plaintext
   env://GITHUB_TOKEN
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All active repositories** or **Select repositories to monitor**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>
